rationale: Economics & Finance has the JEL classification as a reference, but the modern field has shifted: empirical micro is now huge, behavioral has matured, and macro is in a methodological revolution. Fourteen subspecialties split mostly by topical field (labor, public, health, etc.) plus methodological homes (econometrics, behavioral). I made Behavioral & Experimental its own card because the experimental economics community has a sharp identity. I kept Health Economics under Public Health at level 1, so it's NOT here — papers go either to PH (population-health angle) or here under Health & Labor Economics (labor-market angle).

subspecialties.name: Microeconomic Theory
subspecialties.tagline: Game theory, mechanism design, contract theory, market design.
covers: game theory, mechanism design, contract theory, market design, decision theory, social choice theory
Theor Econ, J Econ Theory, Games Econ Behav

subspecialties.name: Macroeconomics
subspecialties.tagline: Business cycles, monetary, fiscal, growth theory.
covers: business cycles, monetary economics, fiscal policy, growth theory, DSGE models, heterogeneous agent macro (HANK), inflation analysis
AER macro, JME, J Monet Econ
explicitly_excludes: central banking policy → Health Policy related social sciences

subspecialties.name: Econometrics
subspecialties.tagline: Identification, panel data, time series, ML for econ.
covers: identification strategies, panel data methods, time series econometrics, nonparametric and semiparametric methods, ML for econ, structural estimation
Econometrica methods, JOE, JE

subspecialties.name: Labor Economics
subspecialties.tagline: Wages, employment, education returns, immigration, discrimination.
covers: wages and employment, education economics, returns to schooling, immigration economics, gender and racial discrimination economics, automation and labor
JOLE, ILR Rev, JHR

subspecialties.name: Public Economics
subspecialties.tagline: Taxation, welfare programs, public goods, political economy.
covers: optimal taxation, welfare program evaluation, social insurance, public goods provision, political economy of taxation, redistribution
JPubE, AEJ Policy

subspecialties.name: Development Economics
subspecialties.tagline: LMIC, poverty, RCTs in development, growth in LDCs.
covers: development economics, poverty alleviation, RCTs in development, agricultural development, growth in LDCs, microfinance research
J Dev Econ, AEJ Applied, WB Econ Rev

subspecialties.name: Industrial Organization & Competition Policy
subspecialties.tagline: Firm behavior, market structure, antitrust, platforms.
covers: industrial organization, market structure, antitrust economics, platform economics, vertical relationships, regulatory economics
Rand J Econ, IJIO

subspecialties.name: Behavioral & Experimental Economics
subspecialties.tagline: Lab experiments, nudges, prospect theory, field experiments.
covers: lab and field experimental economics, behavioral economics theory, nudge research, prospect theory applications, social preferences
Exp Econ, JEEA, AER Insights

subspecialties.name: Finance & Asset Pricing
subspecialties.tagline: Asset pricing, corporate finance, banking, macro-finance.
covers: asset pricing theory and empirics, corporate finance, banking and intermediation, household finance, real estate finance, macro-finance
JF, RFS, JFE
explicitly_excludes: quantitative finance ML → Computer Science & AI (overlap)

subspecialties.name: International Economics
subspecialties.tagline: Trade, exchange rates, international macro, globalization.
covers: international trade theory and empirics, exchange rates, international macroeconomics, capital flows, globalization research
J Int Econ, AEJ International, JIMF

subspecialties.name: Environmental & Energy Economics
subspecialties.tagline: Climate economics, energy markets, environmental policy economics.
covers: climate economics, integrated assessment models, energy markets, environmental policy economics, natural resource economics, conservation economics
JEEM, JAERE, EER

subspecialties.name: Quantitative Finance & Mathematical Finance
subspecialties.tagline: Derivatives, stochastic vol, market microstructure, HFT.
covers: derivatives pricing, stochastic volatility, market microstructure, high-frequency trading research, optimal execution, risk management quantitative
Math Finance, Quant Finance, arXiv q-fin
explicitly_excludes: pure financial ML → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Economic History
subspecialties.tagline: Long-run growth, historical institutions, archival economics.
covers: long-run economic history, historical institutions, archival economic research, history of business, history of finance
JEH, Explor Econ Hist, Econ Hist Rev

subspecialties.name: Applied Microeconomics & Policy Evaluation
subspecialties.tagline: Education, housing, criminal justice, family economics.
covers: applied microeconomics, education policy evaluation, housing economics, economics of crime, family and household economics, applied policy evaluation
AEJ Applied, JHR applied, AER applied
explicitly_excludes: pure policy without economics → Social Sciences

uncovered_concerns: Considered Insurance Economics as its own card — folds under Public Economics or Finance depending on angle.
Considered Sports Economics — folds under Applied Microeconomics.
Considered Sociology of Markets — folds under Social Sciences.
